preconditions: Error signal detected in logs or output
strategy: Read the full error message and stack trace
Identify the root cause (not just the symptom)
Search for existing capsules matching the error pattern
Apply minimal fix targeting only the broken code
Verify fix with existing tests or manual validation
Record the solution as a new capsule

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: General error repair strategy — fix bugs with minimal changes

strategy: Locate the exact line where the TypeError occurs
Trace back to find where the value becomes null/undefined
Add null checks, optional chaining (?.), or nullish coalescing (??)
Verify the fix doesn't mask a deeper issue
Add type annotations if in TypeScript

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Fix TypeError and null/undefined access errors

strategy: Check if the module is installed (package.json)
Verify the import path is correct (relative vs absolute)
Check for typos in module name
Run npm install if package is missing
Check tsconfig paths if using TypeScript aliases

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Fix module import and require errors

preconditions: Code identified as overly complex or duplicated
strategy: Identify the code section to extract
Determine the right abstraction (function, class, module)
Extract with clear interface and naming
Update all call sites to use the new abstraction
Run tests to verify behavior is preserved
Remove dead code if any

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Extract and decompose complex code into cleaner abstractions

preconditions: Variable, function, or file name identified as misleading or inconsistent
strategy: Identify all usages of the symbol to rename
Choose a name that follows existing conventions
Use IDE/LSP rename for safe refactoring
Update related comments and documentation
Verify no broken references

preconditions: Performance issue identified via profiling, logs, or user report
strategy: Profile to identify the actual bottleneck (don't guess)
Check for N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, or blocking I/O
Apply targeted optimization (caching, batching, lazy loading)
Measure before and after to confirm improvement
Document the optimization rationale

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Performance optimization through profiling and targeted fixes

preconditions: Clear feature requirement or user request
strategy: Understand the full requirement before coding
Check for existing patterns in the codebase to follow
Implement incrementally with tests
Handle edge cases and error states
Update documentation if needed
Record the implementation as a capsule for future reference

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Add new features following existing codebase patterns

strategy: Assess the severity and attack vector
Apply the principle of least privilege
Sanitize inputs, escape outputs
Use parameterized queries for database access
Add security headers if web-facing
Verify the fix doesn't introduce new vulnerabilities
Update dependencies if CVE is in a package

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Fix security vulnerabilities with defense-in-depth approach

preconditions: Code identified as lacking test coverage or having flaky tests
strategy: Identify the critical paths that need testing
Write tests for happy path first, then edge cases
Use existing test patterns and frameworks in the project
Ensure tests are deterministic (no flaky tests)
Aim for meaningful assertions, not just coverage numbers

metadata.author: seed
metadata.description: Add missing tests and fix flaky tests

strategy: Read the first error in the build output (subsequent errors may cascade)
Check for TypeScript type errors (TS2xxx codes)
Verify imports and exports match
Check tsconfig.json for configuration issues
Run npm install if dependencies changed
Fix one error at a time, rebuild after each fix
